purpose: Uses the tension between two historical analogues (1997-2000 market structure + 1966-69 macro) to capture competing forces in the current environment
components: 1997-2000 analogue: extreme concentration, transformative technology narrative, accommodative Fed
1966-69 analogue: fiscal dominance, inflation not vanquished, premature accommodation, nominal masking real deterioration
Tension: one implies blow-off top (1997-2000), other implies grinding inflationary bear (1966-69)
Key differentiator: household balance sheets materially stronger today
Resolution depends on which force dominates
current_assessment: Both analogues fit current conditions on their respective dimensions. The 1997-2000 fit captures market structure (AI concentration, tech narrative). The 1966-69 fit captures macro backdrop (fiscal dominance, persistent inflation). Stronger household balance sheets may extend the cycle beyond what either analogue suggests.
limitations: Dual-analogue approach is more nuanced but still limited by historical specificity
No method to weight relative importance of market structure vs macro backdrop
Household balance sheet differentiator may not be sufficient to alter outcomes
Both analogues ultimately ended badly, differing mainly in mechanism
